nouns.summary: Josiah is stated to have “begotten”/given birth to the child mentioned in the text (Ἰεχονίαν), with the birth occurring during the time described as Babylon’s deportation.
Jeconiah is named as the person whom Josiah begot/gave birth to, and this birth is placed in the timeframe of Babylon’s deportation.
Josiah is also said to have begotten Jeconiah’s brothers, indicating that multiple brothers are born as part of the same event/time referenced by the text (the deportation of Babylon).
The text references a deportation/resettlement (exile) as the circumstance under which Josiah’s birth of Jeconiah and his brothers is said to occur, linking their birth to that historical event.
Babylon is identified as the location associated with the deportation/resettlement mentioned in the text, i.e., the brothers’ and Jeconiah’s birth is tied to the deportation to Babylon.
